rulings.Case Title: 55-55A-57-57A-59-59A-61-63-63A WOODWARD STREET TIC VS. KEVIN LEHANE ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Notice Of Lhjs Investments, Llcs Motion For Summary Judgment Or Summary Adjudication, In The Alternative
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, November 1, 2018, Line 1, DEFENDANT LHJS INVESTMENTS, LLC's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or Summary Adjudication, In The Alternative Defendant LHJS Investment LLC's motion for summary judgment or alternatively summary adjudication on the claims alleged against it in the third amended complaint is denied in its entirety. LHJS fails to maintain its burden because it does not address the alter ego allegations against it. There is also a triable issue whether LHJS negligently converted the premises per Orange Grove Terrace Owners Ass'n v. Bryant Properties, Inc. (1986) 176 Cal.App.3d 1217. Mr. Simonse declares that LHJS performed no contractor or developer activities. Plaintiff presents sufficient evidence to create a triable issue of fact regarding that issue. Contractors made requests for payment directly from LHJS, who is listed as the owner of the project. (Plaintiff's Ex. 4, 00006.) Ronald Paz acted as LJHS's construction manager and he approved Lehane's bills. (Plaintiff's Ex. 4, 00006; Ex. 5, 000002.) The managing member of LHJS (John Simonse) sent an e-mail criticizing and directing the work. (Plaintiff's Ex. 6, 001.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for plaintiff is required to prepare a proposed order in compliance with CCP 437c(g) which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/hk

rulings.Case Title: 55-55A-57-57A-59-59A-61-63-63A WOODWARD STREET TIC VS. KEVIN LEHANE ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Notice Of Motion And Motion For Summary Judgment Or In The Alternative Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, November 1, 2018, Line 1, DEFENDANT MAGNATE FUND #2 LLC's Motion For Summary Judgment Or In The Alternative Summary Adjudication Defendant Magnate Fund #2, LLC and LHJS Investments, LLC's motion for summary judgment or alternatively summary adjudication on the claims alleged against them in the third amended complaint is denied in its entirety. There is a triable issue whether Magnate and LHJS acted in a conventional role as a mere lender of money or whether they actively participated in the project such that they can be liable for negligence per Daniels v. Select Portfolio Servicing, Inc. (2016) 246 Cal.App.4th 1150, 1181. There is also a triable issue regarding alter ego liability. Movants present evidence that they acted as lenders and there is a separate identity among the entities. (Simonse dec., pars. 2-13.) Plaintiff's controverting evidence creates triable issues of material fact. The evidence discloses that LHJS is the manager of Magnate and 55 Woodward. (Defendants' UMF 4, Simonse Dec., par. 14.) Contractors made requests for payment directly from LHJS, who is listed as the owner of the project. (Plaintiff's Ex. 4, 00006.) The managing member of LHJS (John Simonse) sent an e-mail criticizing and directing the work. (Plaintiff's Ex. 6, 001.) Ronald Paz acted as LJHS's construction manager and he approved Lehane's bills (Plaintiff's Ex. 4, 00006) and coordinated the work (Plaintiff's Ex. 21, 003, 005, 007, 015, 0021, 0068.) He even weighed in on whether to change the paint color of the project. (Plaintiff's Ex. 21, 005.) Magnate acquired title on 6/18/10 (Plaintiff's Ex. 14, 003537) and transferred title to 55 Woodward on 6/22/10 (Plaintiff's Ex. 15, 003542). No transfer tax was paid. (Plaintiff's Ex. 15, 003542.) Prior to transferring title, Magnate hired Vierra Moore to prepare TIC documents. (Plaintiff's Ex. 16, 000093.) Simonse (on behalf of 55 Woodward, Magnate, and LHJS) submitted a supplemental questionnaire to the SFDRE regarding converting the property to a TIC. (Plaintiff's Ex. 2, 000128-129.) 55 Woodward and Magnate never had any employees. (Plaintiff's Ex.17, Simonse Depo., 21:9-11.) Magnate, 55 Woodward, and LHJS all shared the same address. (Plaintiff's Ex. 19.) David Waite is managing member of LHJS and had authority to act for Magnate with regard to selling properties. (Plaintiff's Ex. 9, 003536.) There is no information about the amount or type of insurance that 55 Woodward obtained. (Defendants' UMF 15.) 55 Woodward no longer exists. (Defendants' UMF 20.) A reasonable trier of fact could conclude that defendants were actively involved in the construction project, there is a unity of interest among the parties, and respecting the corporate/LLC form could lead to an inequitable result. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for plaintiff is required to prepare a proposed order in compliance with CCP 437c(g) which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested.

rulings.Case Title: 55-55A-57-57A-59-59A-61-63-63A WOODWARD STREET TIC VS. KEVIN LEHANE ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Defendant 55 Woodward Llcs Notice Of Motion And Motion For Summary Judgment Or In The Alternative Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, November 1, 2018, Line 1, Defendant 55 Woodward Llcs' Motion For Summary Judgment Or In The Alternative Summary Adjudication Defendant 55 Woodward, LLC's motion for summary judgment or alternatively summary adjudication on the claims alleged against it in the third amended complaint is denied in its entirety. 55 Woodward fails to demonstrate that it cannot be liable for the negligent conversion of the property per Orange Grove Terrace Owners Ass'n v. Bryant Properties, Inc. (1986) 176 Cal.App.3d 1217. 55 Woodward does not sufficiently address plaintiff's allegations of alter ego liability and that 55 Woodward, Magnate, and LHJS negligently renovated the project and caused damage to plaintiffs. Plaintiff, a TIC, has standing to maintain a claim for breach of contract. (CCP 382 [representative capacity]; Raven's Cove Townhomes, Inc. v. Knuppe Development Co. (1981) 114 Cal.App.3d 783; Market Lofts Community Association v. 9th Street Market Lofts, LLC (2014) 222 Cal.App.4th 924, 932.) Lastly, there is a triable issue of material fact regarding whether the claims are time barred per CCP 338 as Mr. Paz was remedying problems on a punch list through at least 8/24/12, which was three years before plaintiff filed the action. (Plaintiff's Ex. 5.) The court overrules 55 Woodward's objection to the declaration of counsel Mr. Konini as he has knowledge regarding the attached documents through his participation in this litigation. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for plaintiff is required to prepare a proposed order in compliance with CCP 437c(g) which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/hk

rulings.Case Title: ALBERT SHIH ET AL VS. YI TAN ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Vacate Default And Default Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, November 1, 2018, Line 3, DEFENDANT YI TAN's Motion To Vacate Default And Default Judgment Defendant Yi Tan's motion to vacate default and default judgment is granted. The default entered against Mr. Tan on April 18, 2018 and the default judgment entered against him on July 17, 2018 are set aside per CCP 473.5 and 473(b). Mr. Tan has shown that he did not have actual knowledge of this lawsuit in time to file a timely responsive pleading and that, even if there were communications directed to him about the lawsuit prior to entry of the default, his failure to realize the nature of those communications and his failure to respond to the complaint was a result of mistake, inadvertence, surprise and/or excusable neglect. Plaintiffs have not provided persuasive evidence rebutting Mr. Tan's denial of knowledge about this case prior to entry of the default judgment and his denials of making any efforts to evade service of the summons and complaint. Granting this motion comports with California's strong policy of deciding cases on their merits and plaintiffs will not suffer legal prejudice to their ability to prosecute their claims by the granting of the motion. The court does not rule on and reserves the issue of whether some or all of the costs incurred by plaintiffs up to this point in this case should be borne by Mr. Tan. Plaintiffs are permitted to pursue discovery seeking information about Mr. Tan's knowledge of this case prior to the entry of the default judgment and any efforts by Mr. Tan to evade service of the summons and complaint. If plaintiffs believe they have compelling evidence that Mr. Tan knew about this case prior to the entry of the default judgment and/or made efforts to evade service of the summons and complaint, in addition to seeking reconsideration of this order or inviting the court to reconsider the order, they may also seek an award of some or all of the costs they incurred up to this point in the case. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Mr. Tan is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/hk

rulings.Case Title: MARCELA MARTINEZ VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice And Motion To Compel Further Complete Verified Discovery Responses Of Defendants Another Planet Entertainment Llc Ccsf And Tida To Set 1 Form Interrogatories Special Interrogatories And Requests For Production
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, November 1, 2018, Line 1, PLAINTIFF MARCELA MARTINEZ'S Motion To Compel Further Complete Verified Discovery Responses Of Defendants Another Planet Entertainment LLC, CCSF And Tida To Set 1 Form Interrogatories Special Interrogatories And Requests For Production Pro Tem Judge David McDonald, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Motion granted in part and denied in part. The motion is granted as to Form Interrogatory No. 15.1 and Interrogatory No. 2. The supplemental responses are to be provided within 21 days. As to the RFPDs, the Court finds that the written responses are code compliant. To the extent that any responsive, non-privileged documents have not been produced, Defendants are ordered to produce them within 21 days. Any documents withheld on privilege must be placed on a log. As to the 911 tape, Defendant CCSF shall produce the tape subject to a protective order. The parties are directed to meet and confer about the terms of the protective order. If an agreement cannot be reached, Defendant shall submit a proposed order to the Court and Plaintiff may file any objections. Sanctions denied. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to davidmcdonald@prodigy.net with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: DOUGLAS N AKAY VS. MARTHE SCHREIBER ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT / Notice
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, November 1, 2018, Line 4, PLAINTIFF DOUGLAS AKAY's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Plaintiff Douglas Akay's motion for summary judgment on any or all of the four causes of action alleged in his first amended complaint is denied in its entirety. The evidence submitted by defendant Marthe Schreiber creates triable disputes whether some or all of the money claimed by Mr. Akay is owed by Ms. Schreiber. That evidence shows that there are material disputes whether: 1) Mr. Akay had the authority to perform some of the services he performed for which he is seeking damages; and 2) whether there was any value and, if so, the extent of the value of some of the services performed by Mr. Akay for which he is seeking damages. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Ms. Schreiber is required to prepare a proposed order which complies with CCP 437c(g) and repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/hk

If the tentative ruling adversely affects, in whole or in part, any party who is not represented by an attorney, then any other party (whether represented by an attorney or not) may submit on the tentative ruling without appearing for the hearing only if the adversely affected self-represented party has stated in writing or orally that it, he or she will not appear at the hearing. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the court will sign the proposed order and proposed judgment submitted with the moving papers.

rulings.Case Title: SARA SCORSONELLI VS. DOROTEA GIANNINI ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Pl.S Motion Discovery Of Financial Condition And Declaration Of Plaintiff
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, November 1, 2018, Line 2, PLAINTIFF SARA SCORSONELLI'S Motion Discovery Of Financial Condition And Declaration Of Plaintiff Pro Tem Judge David McDonald, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Motion denied. The Court finds that Plaintiff's declaration alone does not establish there is a substantial probability that she will prevail on the claim for punitive damages. Rivera v. Sassoon (1995) 39 Cal. App. 4th 1045. Moreover, many of the statements in Plaintiff's declaration are inadmissible hearsay and Plaintiff has not included in her motion any evidence that collaborates the allegations in her declaration. The ruling is without prejudice to it being revisited by the trial judge if there is a finding that Defendants acted with malice or oppression. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to davidmcdonald@prodigy.net with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: MADELINE I TURSKEY VS. ALLY FINANCIAL, INC. A DELAWARE CORPORATION ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion For Preliminary Injunctiion And Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, November 1, 2018, Line 10, PLAINTIFF MADELINE TURSKEY's Motion For Preliminary Injunctiion Plaintiff Madeline Turskey's motion for preliminary injunction is granted in part. Until final judgment or a contrary order is entered in this case, defendant Ally Financial, Inc. and all persons and entities working in concert with them are restrained and enjoined from refusing to accept any payments on-line from any persons who have previously signed up and been approved to make on-line payments due to any disputes with those persons or Ally having been contacted by any attorneys on behalf of those persons without providing 30 days advance notice directly to those persons and stating how those persons may make their payments without incurring any additional costs. This public injunction is necessary to ensure that persons who have been approved to make on-line payments are not misled to their detriment. All requested injunctive relief seeking greater restraints on Ally are denied because it has not been sufficiently shown that such relief is necessary to ensure compliance with Business and Professions Code 17200. Because it is a public injunction, the court has the authority to issue it notwithstanding any arbitration language in Ally's Terms of Service. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Ms. Turskey is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/hk

rulings.Case Title: EUGENE SORIANO VS. ARXAN TECHNOLOGIES, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, November 1, 2018, Line 13, DEFENDANT ARXAN TECHNOLOGIES, INC. DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT Defendant Arxan Technologies, Inc's demurrer to all ten causes of action in the complaint filed by plaintiff Eugene Soriano is overruled as to all ten causes of action. The face of the complaint and the judicially noticeable separation agreement do not necessarily show that any or all of Mr. Soriano's claims are barred by the release language in the separation agreement. Liberally construed, Mr. Soriano's allegations are sufficient to support economic duress as grounds for rescission of the separation agreement. "The question of duress. . .is a factual question; the existence of duress always depends upon the circumstances" and it requires an examination of "the state of mind induced thereby in the victim. The means used to produce that condition, the age, sex, state of health, and mental characteristics of the alleged injured party, are all evidentiary, merely, of the ultimate fact in issue, of whether such person was bereft of the free exercise of his will power." (Philippine Export & Foreign Loan Guarantee Corp. v. Chuidian (1990) 218 Cal.App.3d 1058, 1078 (emphasis in the original)). Liberally construed, the complaint alleges that at the time he signed the separation agreement Mr. Soriano was suffering from hyperglycemia and was severely distraught and traumatized by his abusive work environment, including the statement that Mr. Soriano was compelled to quit his employment notwithstanding that he had almost six months left on his oral agreement and the grabbing of Mr. Soriano's office equipment while he was being threatened to be physically dragged out of the building with the shouting of "do you want me to call security?" Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Mr. Soriano is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/hk
